programmes.show_description: Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
2/12. Ally is hoping to make his mark at Dingwall Mart, Adam and Lucy are under huge pressure to make the big move earlier than expected, and winter storms cost the Glenn family dearly.
5/6. The Isle of Mull Rugby Club hire Banjo to redesign their bar. But with little knowledge of the game, how will Banjo's adventurous design go down with the club?
6/6. To launch a new spirit distilled on the cheese farm where he lives, Banjo is asked to design a rustic gin tasting bar, using salvaged materials found on the farm.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Bringing you more from the day's top stories and communities across Scotland.
Sarah is the fourth generation of her family to 'take up the saw', working across the forests of the Central Highlands of Scotland with her family.
5/8. There is competition for a job on the Fife caravan park, and Gibby and Josh from the maintenance team are keen to impress. Meanwhile, manager Steven is challenged to a game of bowls.
6/8. Manager Steven juggles Fife caravan park duties with volunteering at the RNLI station, a resident takes to the water, and there is a new recruit in the kitchen.
1/6. Banjo and his team are called in to give the Puffer Pub a makeover on the tiny island of Easdale. Can they complete the work ahead of the World Stone Skimming Championships?
2/6. Banjo and his team have been tasked with making over an apothecary on Harris. The large, cavernous space will need completely renovating on a tiny budget.
A well-meaning Scarlett accidentally causes a rift between Bob and Alice, but when Sam steps in, it confirms her newfound feelings.
4/6. Cathy invites the neighbours round to celebrate Colin's dad's birthday. Ceilidh! Contains some strong language and adult humour.
Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.
5/6. Limmy suggests a well-placed mime to get you out of some pretty serious scrapes. Contains some strong language and adult humour.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Highlights from the BBC Scotland channel.
Bringing you more from the day's top stories and communities across Scotland.
3/6. Following the people working at Aberdeen harbour. Trainee harbour pilot Ralph faces a tough test on the simulator. Ex-sailor Ellen hopes to take Ralph's place as VTS controller.
3/12. Zara worries that Callum's accident has had a lasting effect, Emma and Rob call in the vet for a difficult birth, and Ally and Bethany set sail for an island sheep gather.
How grieving parents forced the government to ban handguns after the Dunblane shooting. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.
3/3. Documentary series examining the political changes that shaped modern-day Scotland. Scotland's ideological struggle in the wake of World War I.
Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.
Criminologist David Wilson examines the complex topic of terrorism. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
